semantic.role: Standardized template for submitting pull requests to the ArchSpine project.
responsibilities: Providing a structured format for describing changes (what and why).
Linking related issues or feature requests.
Categorizing the type of change (bug fix, new feature, breaking change, documentation, performance).
Enforcing a pre-submission checklist (style, tests, documentation, warnings).
Allowing optional screenshots to illustrate visual or functional changes.
outOfScope: Detailed project coding guidelines or style conventions.
Step-by-step contribution workflow outside the template itself.
Repository-specific branch or commit naming rules.
Long-form project documentation or architecture descriptions.

English.purpose: To ensure all pull requests follow a consistent format and include necessary information for review, thereby streamlining the contribution process.
English.context_and_audience: This template is intended for developers and contributors who are submitting pull requests to the ArchSpine repository. It provides a clear structure that helps both the author and the reviewers by standardizing the information provided in each PR.
key_takeaways: Every PR must include a description of the change and the reason it was needed.
A checklist ensures that contributors self-review, run tests, and update documentation before submission.
Change type must be explicitly classified (bug, feature, breaking, docs, performance).
Including screenshots is optional but recommended for changes that affect the user interface or behavior.
